name: Royal Chocolate Torte
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 10-inch springform pan; set aside. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with mixer on high speed until soft peaks form. Gradually add in sugar (1/4 cup at a time), beating well after each addition until sugar is completely dissolved. Whites should stand in stiff, glossy peaks.
In a small bowl with mixer on medium speed, beat egg yolks, vanilla, and 3/4 cup sugar until thick and lemon-colored. Beat in flour and cocoa at low speed; gently fold into the egg yolk mixture. Pour batter into prepared pan and spread evenly.
Bake 35-40 minutes or until the top of the cake springs back when lightly touched with a finger. Remove from oven and cool completely on a wire rack.
In a small bowl, beat pudding mix, milk, almond extract, and 1 cup heavy cream with mixer on medium speed until stiff peaks form (about 5 minutes). Remove cake from pan bottom; cut horizontally into 3 even layers using a serrated knife. Place one layer on a serving platter; spread with half of the pudding mixture. Arrange second layer on top; spread remaining pudding mixture; place the third layer on top. Refrigerate.
In a double boiler over hot, not boiling, water, heat rum and chocolate pieces until smooth. In a small bowl, beat heavy cream and chocolate mixture with mixer on medium high speed until thick (about 15 minutes). Use to frost sides and top of torte; refrigerate.
For the chocolate disk: Melt remaining chocolate chips and shortening in double boiler over hot water for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Spread melted chocolate evenly on waxed paper-lined springform pan bottom; refrigerate until firm (about 10 minutes). Carefully remove chocolate disk from pan bottom using cool hands; wipe knife dry. Cut chocolate into a rectangle using a sharp knife.
Cut torte into wedges and cut each strawberry in half. Place a strawberry half, cut side down, on the center of each wedge; lean a chocolate wedge on top of the strawberry.
